title: Electroporation of the protist Cafeteria roenbergensis cells using the Neon Transfection System
steps: Before the experiment (it can be the day before without affecting too much the cell viability) Density-based removal of bacteria from a culture of the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis by Sarah Duponchel
Centrifuge the Cafeteria cells at 4,500 g, 5 min, room temperature
Wash twice the cell pellet with 1X PBS => use small volume of PBS to concentrate the cells

Prepare the different reagents (CaCl 2 and 2X HEBS) according to the followed protocol from Pia Elustondo Calcium phosphate transfection of Oxyrrhis marina with an Alexa488-labelled DNA fragment by Pia Elustondo Aliquot and autoclave the 2X HEBS buffer to avoid contamination

Mix the CaCl 2 (final concentration 125 mM) with the material to electroporate - for DNA electroporation complete to the final volume with water - for protein electroporation complete to the final volume with 1X PBS or the buffer the most suitable for the protein For one electroporation, final volume of the CaCl 2 mixture 10-μl tips: 3.5 μl 100-μl tips: 48.5 μl

Add the CaCl 2 mixture onto the 2X HEBS For one electroporation, 2X HEBS volume 10-μl tips: 5 μl 100-μl tips: 50 μl Option 1: volumes are big enough to vortex constantly the HEBS while the CaCl 2 mixture is added drop by drop Option2: 1/10th of the CaCl 2 mixture is added at a time with a 3-secondes vortexing-step between each addition

Count the cells: stain 10 µL of Cafeteria culture with 1 µL of Lugol’s acid iodine solution and load them in a haemocytometer (Neubauer Chamber) Maximum volume of the desirable number of cells needed for the electroporation = 1.5 μl
Add the 1.5 μl volume of the appropriate number of C. roenbergensis cells to the CaCl 2 /HEBS mixture
Electroporate the cells at 1600V, for 20 ms, 1 pulse using either the 10-μl or 100-μl tips
Place the electroporated cells into 10 time more f/2 media than the electroporated volume for 30 min

Cells can either be fixed or maintained in culture by adding 5 time fresh f/2 media complement with 0.02% yeast extract and 1/500th of E.coli culture Fluorescence microscopy with the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis by Monica Berjon-Otero
creator: Sarah Duponchel
